stem: Two printing machines, A and B, are used to complete a large print job of 1,625 pages. Machine A prints 75 pages per hour and Machine B prints 50 pages per hour. Machine A starts the job alone and works for 5 hours before Machine B joins. Both machines then work together until the job is complete. For how many hours did Machine B work?
explanation: Machine A works alone for 5 hours, printing 75 × 5 = 375 pages. Remaining pages: 1,625 − 375 = 1,250. When both machines work together, their combined rate is 75 + 50 = 125 pages per hour. Time working together: 1,250 ÷ 125 = 10 hours. Since Machine B joins when both work together, Machine B worked for 10 hours.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: Machine A prints 75 × 5 = 375 pages alone; remaining 1,625 − 375 = 1,250 pages at combined rate 125 pages/hour: 1,250 ÷ 125 = 10.
distractorRationale.B: Used Machine B's rate instead of Machine A's rate for the solo period: 50 × 5 = 250 pages; remaining 1,625 − 250 = 1,375; together at 125 pages/hour: 1,375 ÷ 125 = 11.
distractorRationale.C: Ignored Machine A's solo work and divided total pages by the combined rate: 1,625 ÷ 125 = 13.
distractorRationale.D: Reported Machine A's total working time instead of Machine B's: Machine A worked 5 hours alone plus 10 hours together = 15.
